sections.title: Speaking to the Mage of Zamorak in the Wilderness
Speaking to the Mage of Zamorak again

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I already told you! Meet me in the Varrock Chaos Temple!

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Meet me in Varrock's Chaos Temple. Here is not the place to talk.

sections.title: Speaking to the mage in the Chaos Temple
Speaking to the mage in the Chaos Temple with Saradomin/Guthixian items equipped

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: How dare you wear such disrespectful attire in this holy place? Remove them immediately if you wish to speak to me.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Well? Have you managed to use my scrying orb to obtain the information yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... No.... I kind of lost that orb thingy that you gave me...

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: What? Incompetent fool. Take this. And do not make me regret allying myself with you.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I can offer a replacement, but not if you have no room to accept it, fool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No... Actually, I had something I wanted to ask you...

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I assumed the task to be self-explanatory. What is it you wish to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please excuse me, I have a very bad short term memory. What exactly am I supposed to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I am slightly concerned about your capability for this mission, if you cannot even recall such a simple task...

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: All I wish for you to do is to teleport to this 'rune essence' location from three different locations while carrying the scrying orb I gave you. It will collect the data as you teleport, and if you then

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: bring it to me I will be able to use it to further my own investigations.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you want me to teleport to the rune essence mine while carrying the scrying ball?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: That is correct.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I need to teleport into the essence mine from three different locations?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: That is also correct.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... I think I understand that...

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: That is good to know. Is there something else you need clarifying?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just want to know one thing: What's in this for me?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Well now, I can certainly understand that attitude. Let me assure you of this; from what you tell me, you have discovered a way of gaining these 'essence stones' quickly and efficiently, yet it sounds as though your

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: method of reaching the temples which are used to bind these essences is random and counter-productive.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I, on the other hand, have information on quickly accessing these temples, yet my methods of procuring this 'essence' are time consuming and useless as a means of mass production.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I think you can see for yourself how this may benefit both of us; You allow me the knowledge of finding plentiful essence, and I will in return show you the secret of these

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: temples.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, okay, fair enough.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Was there anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I help you with this... I'd just like to keep my conscience clear and know that you're not going to use whatever information I give to you in the pursuit of evil and badness and stuff.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Very well, if it makes you feel any better; I promise you that any knowledge you provide me with, absolutely will not be used in the service of anything more evil than what I was already planning on doing

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: anyway.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I hope that sets your conscience at rest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know... In a weird way it actually does...

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Excellent. Was there anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This crystal ball thingy is kind of nifty. Can I have another one?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: It is not a 'crystal ball thingy'. It is a precision made magical instrument called a scrying orb. Do you have some particular need for another one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really, I just like to fill up my bank with cool looking stuff.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Then no, no you may not have another one. One will be sufficient for the task at hand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... But I lost the one you gave me... Can I have another one then?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: No you haven't. I can see that you have it there in your backpack. Now quit your foolishness and get on with the task in hand!

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: No you haven't. You have it in your bank. Now get on with it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I think I have everything clear in my head now.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: That is good to hear, stranger. Bring me my orb back when you have successfully made those teleports.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure thing, boss.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: And don't call me 'boss'.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Well? Have you managed to use my scrying orb to obtain the information yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I have! I've got it right here!

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Excellent. Give it here, and I shall examine the findings. Speak to me in a small while.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... that's my end of the deal upheld. What do I get in return?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Indeed, a deal is always a deal.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I offer you three things as reward for your efforts on behalf of my Lord Zamorak;

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: The first is knowledge. I offer you my collected research on the abyss. I also offer you 1000 points of experience in Runecrafting for your trouble.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Your second gift is convenience. Here, you may take this pouch I discovered amidst my research. You will find it to have certain... interesting properties.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Your final gift is that of movement. I will from now on offer you a teleport to the abyss whenever you should require it.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Huh? Abyss? What are you talking about? You told me that you would help me with Runecrafting!

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: And so I have done. Read my research notes, they may enlighten you somewhat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... I really don't see how this talk about an 'abyss' relates to Runecrafting in the slightest...

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: My primary research responsibility was not towards the manufacture of runes, this is true.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Rather, I was investigating an error that occurred to one of our initiates in a routine teleportation experiment.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: My discovery was this 'abyssal' space, and as a side effect, as documented in the research notes I have given you, I discovered the existence of these 'temples' used to create runes.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Fortunate for both of us, I would say. As I say, read my research notes, it should all become clear.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: My colleague inside the abyss may be of help to you as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... This 'abyss' place... Is it dangerous?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... This 'abyss' place... Is it dangerous?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Well, the creatures there ARE particularly offensive...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean they smell?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: No, I mean they hunt and attack any visitors to their dimension on sight. This is not the danger however.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: No. Unfortunately the magic we have had to use to retain a portal to the abyss open and effective is derived from Lord Zamorak himself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And that's a bad thing somehow...?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Well, he has his occasional quirks. In this case it means that when you enter this dimension you will be 'skulled' and your prayer will be drained.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: This makes it somewhat more dangerous than other places you may be used to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see...

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Was there anything else you wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I reckon I'm prepared to go there now. Beam me there, or whatever it is that you do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I reckon I'm prepared to go there now. Beam me there, or whatever it is that you do!

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: No, not from here. The use of my Lord Zamoraks magic in this land will draw too much attention to myself.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Meet me in the wilderness where you spoke to me before, and right click to find the 'teleport' option.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I trust you do not wish to have too lengthy a conversation in such a dangerous place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're right, I don't!

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I should be able to improve my stock of runes thanks to your assistance locating the essence site too, so feel free to stop by if you require any specific runes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks!

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Ah, you again. What was it you wanted? The Wilderness is hardly the appropriate place for a conversation now, is it?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I do not conduct business in this pathetic city. Speak to me in the wilderness if you desire to purchase runes from me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, please!

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pretty please with sugar and cherries on top?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: NO!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pleeeeeeeeeease!

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Stranger, I warn you I have a low tolerance for idiocy.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Now are you going to stand here all day annoying me, or was there something that you actually wanted?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, that standing here annoying you thing sounds pretty good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: PLEEEEEEEEEAAAASE? pleasepleasepleaseplease pleasepleasepleaseplease pleasepleasepleaseplease

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Be QUIET or be DEAD!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, what a grouch...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do you get your runes from? No offence, but people around here don't exactly like 'your type'.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: My 'type'? Explain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know... Scary bearded men in dark clothing with unhealthy obsessions with destruction and stuff.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Hmmm. Well, you may be right, the foolish Saradominists that own this pathetic city don't appreciate loyal Zamorakians, it is true.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you can't be getting your runes anywhere around here...

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: That is correct stranger. The mysteries of manufacturing Runes is a closely guarded secret of the Zamorakian brotherhood.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, you mean the whole teleporting to the Rune Essence mine, mining some essence, then using the talismans to locate the Rune Temples, then binding runes there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know all about it...

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: WHAT? I... but... you...

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Tell me, this is important: You know of the ancient temples? You have been to a place where this 'rune essence' material is freely available?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: How did you get to such a place?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I helped deliver some research notes to Sedridor at the Wizards Tower, and he teleported me to a huge mine he said was hidden off to the North somewhere where I could mine essence.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: And there is an abundant supply of this 'essence' there you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but I thought you said that you knew how to make runes? All this stuff is fairly basic knowledge I thought.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: No. No, not at all.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: We occasionally manage to plunder small samples of this 'essence' and we have recently discovered these temples you speak of, but I have never heard of these talismans before, and I was certainly not aware that this 'essence'

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: substance is a heavily stockpiled resource at the Wizards Tower.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: This changes everything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you mean?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: For many years there has been a struggle for power on this world. You may dispute the morality of each side as you wish, but the stalemate that exists between my Lord Zamorak

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: and that pathetic meddling fool Saradomin has meant that our struggles have become more secretive. We exist in a 'cold war' if you will, each side fearful of letting the other gain too much power, and each side

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: equally fearful of entering into open warfare for fear of bringing our struggles to the attention of... other beings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean Guthix?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Indeed. Amongst others. But you now tell me that the Saradominist Wizards have the capability to mass produce runes, I can only

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: conclude that they have been doing so secretly for some time now.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I implore you adventurer, you may or may not agree with my aims, but you cannot allow such a one-sided shift in the balance of power to occur.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Will you help me and my fellow Zamorakians to access this 'essence' mine? In return I will share with you the research we have gathered.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll help you. What can I do?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: All I need from you is the spell that will teleport me to this essence mine. That should be sufficient for the armies of Zamorak to once more begin stockpiling magic for war.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Erm.... I don't actually know that spell.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: What? Then how do you access this location?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, well, people who do know the spell teleport me there directly. Apparently they wouldn't teach it to me to try and keep the location secret.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Hmmm. Yes, yes I see. Very well then, you may still assist us in finding this mysterious essence mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How would I do that?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I'll give you a scrying orb. I have cast a standard cypher spell upon it, so that it will absorb mystical energies that it is exposed to.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Bring it with you and teleport to the rune essence location, and it will absorb the mechanics of the spell and allow us to reverse-engineer the magic behind it.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: The important part is that you must teleport to the essence location from three entirely separate locations.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: More than three may be helpful to us, but we need a minimum of three in order to triangulate the position of this essence mine.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Here's the scrying orb. If you encounter any difficulties speak to me again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I don't want anything to do with you and trying to bribe me into helping isn't going to work on me.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: As you wish, stranger. I will achieve my goals elsewhere.
